text: Code reviews are an important practice in software development. During a code review, a developer reads through a teammate's code to check for errors and suggest improvements. This process is excellent for catching bugs early, before they become bigger problems later on. It also promotes knowledge sharing within the team, as developers learn from each other's approaches and techniques. Over time, this habit improves team quality because everyone becomes more familiar with the codebase and best practices. In short, code reviews help create better software and stronger teams.
